description_long: This object consists of two rectangular, box-like bottles positioned side by side, each with a hollowed-out central cavity visible from the sides. The bottles have a clean, minimalist design with sharp edges and flat surfaces, and their main bodies are predominantly white or light gray. The front and back faces of each bottle appear to be black, possibly indicating an opening or a different material. The interior of each bottle is visible through the side cutouts, revealing a coiled or spiral structure inside, which may serve as a decorative or functional element. The surfaces are smooth and hard, with a matte to slightly glossy finish. There are no visible labels, branding, or cap mechanisms on the exterior surfaces.
description: A pair of rectangular, hollow bottles with side cutouts revealing spiral interiors and contrasting black front and back faces. The design is minimalist, with smooth, hard surfaces and no visible labels or caps.
description_view_0: Front view shows two rectangular bottles with black faces and no visible openings or labels.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the hollowed-out cavities and spiral structures inside each bottle.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the flat, featureless backs of the bottles, similar to the front but without visible openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again shows the hollowed-out interiors and spiral elements, mirroring view 1.
